title: iPSC-Astrocyte Differentiation Protocol
author: Charlie Arber
materials: Passage glial progenitors before they reach 100% confluence.
Feed cells twice per week with maturation media.

steps.title: Remove growth media from cells and...
steps.instruction: Remove growth media from cells and wash once with PBS (without Mg and Ca).

steps.title: Add EDTA (0
steps.instruction: Add EDTA (0.5mM in PBS) for 4-5 minutes at room temperature. Cells will become rounder as the EDTA loosens the cells.

steps.title: Carefully aspirate EDTA and forcefully wash...
steps.instruction: Carefully aspirate EDTA and forcefully wash cells off with fresh glial progenitor media (N2B27 with 10ng/ml FGF2). Continue to pipette cells up and down around 5 times, jetting over the whole growth area.

steps.title: Add glial progenitor cells to new...
steps.instruction: Add glial progenitor cells to new flask/plate at a split ratio of 1:3 to 1:5 in glial progenitor media.

steps.title: Cells may be split once or twice a week
steps.instruction: Cells may be split once or twice a week.

steps.title: After circa day 115, cells will...
steps.instruction: After circa day 115, cells will progress from a neuronal morphology (small round cell body) to a glial morphology (larger and flatter and less defined cell body).

steps.title: Glial progenitors can be maintained and...
steps.instruction: Glial progenitors can be maintained and passaged for over 1 year, but will often slow after DIV 200.

steps.title: Once astrogenic switch has occurred, I...
steps.instruction: Once astrogenic switch has occurred, I have selected day 150 (and passage >15), astrocytes can be matured to astrocytes.

steps.title: Cells can be counted to around 25,000 per cm 2
steps.instruction: Cells can be counted to around 25,000 per cm 2

steps.title: Follow established protocols to generate cortical...
steps.instruction: Follow established protocols to generate cortical neurons, such as Shi et al Nat Protocols (PMID 22976355) until DIV 60 - DIV 80

steps.title: Coat plasticware with Geltrex (1:100 with...
steps.instruction: Coat plasticware with Geltrex (1:100 with DMEM-F12) for 1 hour at 37 degrees (e.g. 6 well plate or T25/T75 flask).

steps.title: Change media to Astrocyte maturation media...
steps.instruction: Change media to Astrocyte maturation media (BMP4+LIF) for at least 2 weeks of maturation. When BMP4+LIF is added, expect cell division reduction. BMP4+LIF for more than 4 weeks is not recommended.

steps.title: For astrocyte activation
steps.instruction: For astrocyte activation: 16 hour treatment with IL1α - 3ng/ml. TNFα – 30ng/ml. C1q – 400ng/ml.

steps.title: Take validation steps such as glutamate...
steps.instruction: Take validation steps such as glutamate uptake, phagocytosis, response to inflammation (nuclear NFkB by ICC, C3 protein production, inflammatory cytokine production by qPCR or ELISA) can be tested to provide evidence of astrocytic identity. Calcium imaging can be used to measure astrocyte functional responses.

steps.title: At this stage (and from now...
steps.instruction: At this stage (and from now on), GPCs can be cryopreserved and stored using standard procedures (i.e. 10% DMSO in N2B27 media).
